title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Archibald Bum suggests to Inspector Sharp that Celia Austin’s death was staged to look like a suicide. He posits that someone living in an adjacent room could have entered her room by jumping across the windows facing the chimney. Mrs. Haberdasher identifies Nigel Bateson as the neighbor. Archibald reveals that Chandra Lal had previously reported that Nigel Bateson took his boric acid. This leads to the realization that someone stole morphine from Nigel’s drawer and replaced it with boric acid; Patricia Lane subsequently switched the boric acid for baking soda, mistakenly believing it was morphine. Sharp shows Mrs. Haberdasher two red strands of hair found in a package, which serves as a clue pointing toward Lennie Bateson.

Later, Hercule Poirot and Inspector Sharp discuss the case, noting that the investigation is progressing. Sharp prepares to go to 26 Walnut Street while Inspector Cobbe handles that location.

Inspector Cobbe and Detective Constable McRae search a beauty salon in Mayfair owned by Mrs. Lucas to investigate the office of Miss Valerie Hobhouse. While searching a safe and desk, Cobbe discovers six blue leather-bound passports taped to the bottom of a desk drawer. The passports feature photos of dark-skinned women using various hairstyles and disguises, representing different identities: Mrs. Da Silva, Miss Irene Frank, Mrs. Olga Cohen, Miss Nina Le Meur, Mrs. Gladys Thomas, and Miss Moira O'Neill. Two additional foreign passports for Madame Mahamoudi and Miss Sheila Donovan are also found. Cobbe concludes these identities were used for smuggling and money laundering through a Mayfair gambling club to avoid taxes, suggesting that Silvia may have seen one of these fake passports dropped on Mountbatten Street.
